Artificial_Intelligence.heading: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence
Artificial_Intelligence.definition: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is a branch of computer science that enables machines to perform tasks that normally require human intelligence such as learning, reasoning, problem-solving, and decision-making.
key_points: Foundation for machine learning and deep learning

Artificial_Intelligence.heading: Intelligent Agents
Artificial_Intelligence.definition: An intelligent agent is a system that perceives its environment using sensors and acts upon it using actuators to achieve goals.
key_points: PEAS framework is used for design

Artificial_Intelligence.heading: Search Algorithms
Artificial_Intelligence.definition: Search algorithms are techniques used in AI to explore problem spaces and find solutions.
key_points: BFS and DFS are basic methods

Artificial_Intelligence.heading: Machine Learning
Artificial_Intelligence.definition: Machine Learning is a subset of AI that enables systems to learn patterns from data without explicit programming.

Artificial_Intelligence.heading: Deep Learning
Artificial_Intelligence.definition: Deep Learning is a subset of machine learning based on neural networks with multiple hidden layers.

Artificial_Intelligence.heading: Neural Networks
Artificial_Intelligence.definition: Neural networks are computational models inspired by the human brain that learn patterns from data.

Artificial_Intelligence.heading: Transformers
Artificial_Intelligence.definition: Transformers are deep learning architectures that use self-attention mechanisms to process sequential data efficiently.

Artificial_Intelligence.heading: GPT Models
Artificial_Intelligence.definition: GPT (Generative Pre-trained Transformer) models are transformer-based language models designed for text generation.

Artificial_Intelligence.heading: Natural Language Processing
Artificial_Intelligence.definition: Natural Language Processing (NLP) enables computers to understand, process, and generate human language.

Artificial_Intelligence.heading: Text Preprocessing
Artificial_Intelligence.definition: Text preprocessing is the process of cleaning and preparing text data for NLP tasks.
key_points: Converts raw text into usable form
Includes tokenization and stop word removal

Artificial_Intelligence.heading: Vectorization
Artificial_Intelligence.definition: Vectorization converts text into numerical representations for machine learning models.

Artificial_Intelligence.heading: Generative AI
Artificial_Intelligence.definition: Generative AI refers to AI systems capable of generating text, images, audio, and other content.

Artificial_Intelligence.heading: Prompt Engineering
Artificial_Intelligence.definition: Prompt engineering is the practice of designing effective prompts to guide AI model responses.

Artificial_Intelligence.heading: RAG Systems
Artificial_Intelligence.definition: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) combines information retrieval with language generation for accurate AI responses.

Artificial_Intelligence.heading: AI Agents and Multi-Agent Systems
Artificial_Intelligence.definition: AI agents are autonomous systems that can reason, plan, and execute tasks independently or collaboratively.
